Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Vectral Systems stipend records. Key discoveries: 5 false rejections found by Dmitri's audit (Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet setup fee, Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 Mesh WiFi System). Agent should check whether the Approval Status on these records has been corrected from "Rejected" to "Approved" after the manual corrections by Mina.
OE 2: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Mosaic Robotics stipend records. Key discoveries: 3 false approvals found by Dmitri's audit (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk exceeding $800 policy limit, Andre Washington $350 coworking space membership — coworking excluded from Mosaic's policy, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes — same exclusion issue). Agent should check whether these records have been corrected. Also: 3 records flagged for stipend fraud (STIP-2026-0089, 0094, 0097 with duplicate receipt hashes). Marcus placed admin holds on STIP-2026-0094 ($89) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45); STIP-2026-0089 ($89, Kevin Tran) was already paid out before discovery. These are a separate issue from the config bugs.
OE 3: Search Linear for all ExpenseBot remediation tickets using linear_list_issues with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" (or similar). Key discoveries: ENG-211 (pilot monitoring, Samira, in_progress), ENG-212 (edge case coverage, Dmitri, in_progress), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-216 (full audit, Dmitri, in_progress, P0), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, Lena, in_progress), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, Anh, in_progress, due 3/21 — overdue), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, Samira, to_do), ENG-220 (duplicate detection, Dmitri, to_do).
OE 4: Search Linear for comments on remediation tickets using linear_list_comments to find deployment status updates. Key discoveries: Samira's pause confirmation on ENG-211 with the full remediation plan and due dates, Lena's deployment verification on ENG-215 showing config corrections deployed but Kevin Tran's internet setup fee remains an edge case.
OE 5: Search Slack #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "ENG-218", "type validation", or "relaunch" (or similar), or browse recent messages via conversations_history on channel C003. Key discovery: Lena's April 22 message confirms ENG-218 is only partially deployed — numeric validation for max_amount works but the array validation for excluded_categories still has the single-item edge case that caused Mosaic's false approvals. Backend is patched (ENG-214/215) but frontend form still produces malformed format. This is a relaunch blocker.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "audit" or "47 expenses" or "accuracy" or "ExpenseBot" (or similar). Key discovery: Samira's email to Elena summarizing Dmitri's audit (83% accuracy, 47 expenses processed, 5 false rejections at Vectral, 3 false approvals at Mosaic, $885 total exposure).
OE 7: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "financial exposure" or "out-of-policy" or "Mosaic" (or similar). Key discovery: Marcus's email flags $1,550 in out-of-policy Mosaic expenses auto-approved ($1,200 standing desk + $350 coworking). Samira's audit email revises this to $885 total exposure ($400 desk overage above $800 cap + $350 coworking excluded + $135 Kenji Nakamura coworking day passes excluded). Also search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "stipend" (or similar) to find admin holds placed by Marcus on duplicate-receipt items (STIP-2026-0094 $89, STIP-2026-0097 $45).
OE 8: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira." Key email: samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 9: Update Linear tickets (via linear_update_issue) where the status doesn't match reality. Key updates: ENG-214/215 (Lena's config corrections deployed and verified, should be "done" if still "in_progress"), ENG-216 (Dmitri completed the full 47-expense audit, should be "done" if still "in_progress"), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening deployed by Lena as part of remediation, should be "done" if still "in_progress").
OE 10: Post a readiness summary in #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message covering: which tickets are done vs still open, the ENG-218 frontend edge case as a relaunch blocker, and overall readiness assessment.
OE 11: Send email (via send_email) from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com summarizing: which tickets are done and which are still open, the ENG-218 edge case Lena flagged, Airtable stipend record accuracy status, QuickBooks financial exposure, and recommendation on re-enabling auto-decisions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Vectral Systems stipend records. OE 2: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 for Mosaic Robotics stipend records. 2 The email to samira.tariq@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) identifies that ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) is still incomplete and flags the excluded_categories array edge case as a relaunch blocker
outcome
 ✓—✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly states 'ENG-218 — Dashboard array validation (my ticket)' and explains the frontend config editor UI 'still serializes single-item excluded_categories as a string instead of a JSON array', calling it a blocker. This directly addresses the excluded_categories array edge case as a relaunch blocker.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The email body explicitly mentions ENG-218, the excluded_categories serialization bug (single-item serializes as string instead of array), and flags it as 'a relaunch blocker'.

✓Run #5The send_email content explicitly mentions 'ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation — my ticket)', the 'excluded_categories array validation still has the single-item edge case', and flags it as a relaunch blocker. The email body directly describes the edge case where a single-item category 'serializes as a string instead of an array — the exact same bug pattern that caused the Mosaic false approvals.'
✓Run #6The email body explicitly mentions ENG-218 as incomplete, describes the excluded_categories array edge case (single-item serialization as string), calls it a potential relaunch concern, and recommends a dashboard editing restriction as mitigation until the fix ships.
3 The email to samira.tariq@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) reports the audit accuracy figure of 83% (or 39 correct out of 47 expenses)
outcome
 ✗—✗✗✓✓ 40%

—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3The email content does not mention the 83% accuracy figure or 39 correct out of 47 expenses. While 47 total expenses are mentioned, no accuracy percentage is stated in the email body.

✓Run #5The email content includes '47 expenses reviewed: 39 correct, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic)' and also references 'ENG-216 (Full pilot audit) — 39 correct', which implicitly gives the 83% figure (39/47). The #engineering Slack message explicitly says 'Results: 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic).' However the email itself says '39 correct' and '47 expenses' but does not explicitly state '83%' — the numbers are there but the percentage itself is not in the email.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly states '47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct (83%), 5 false denials (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic).' All three figures (83%, 39 correct, 47 total) are present.

8 The final response identifies the 5 Vectral false rejections from Dmitri's audit: Danielle Osei ($475 ergonomic chair), Kevin Tran ($185 internet setup fee), Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system).
